218:   @Test
219:   def testJoinGroupShouldReceiveErrorIfGroupOverMaxSize(): Unit = {
220:     val futures = ArrayBuffer[Future[JoinGroupResult]]()
221:     val rebalanceTimeout = GroupInitialRebalanceDelay * 2
222: 
223:     for (i <- 1.to(GroupMaxSize)) {
224:       futures += sendJoinGroup(groupId, JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID, protocolType, protocols, rebalanceTimeout = rebalanceTimeout)
225:       if (i != 1)
226:         timer.advanceClock(GroupInitialRebalanceDelay)
227:       EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
228:     }
229:     // advance clock beyond rebalanceTimeout
230:     timer.advanceClock(GroupInitialRebalanceDelay + 1)
231:     for (future <- futures) {
232:       assertEquals(Errors.NONE, await(future, 1).error)
233:     }
234: 
235:     // Should receive an error since the group is full
236:     val errorFuture = sendJoinGroup(groupId, JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID, protocolType, protocols, rebalanceTimeout = rebalanceTimeout)
237:     assertEquals(Errors.GROUP_MAX_SIZE_REACHED, await(errorFuture, 1).error)
238:   }

155: /**
156:  * Case class used to represent group metadata for the ListGroups API
157:  */
158: case class GroupOverview(groupId: String,
159:                          protocolType: String,
160:                          state: String)

162: /**
163:  * Case class used to represent group metadata for the DescribeGroup API
164:  */
165: case class GroupSummary(state: String,
166:                         protocolType: String,
167:                         protocol: String,
168:                         members: List[MemberSummary])

170: /**
171:   * We cache offset commits along with their commit record offset. This enables us to ensure that the latest offset
172:   * commit is always materialized when we have a mix of transactional and regular offset commits. Without preserving
173:   * information of the commit record offset, compaction of the offsets topic itself may result in the wrong offset commit
174:   * being materialized.
175:   */
176: case class CommitRecordMetadataAndOffset(appendedBatchOffset: Option[Long], offsetAndMetadata: OffsetAndMetadata) {
177:   def olderThan(that: CommitRecordMetadataAndOffset): Boolean = appendedBatchOffset.get < that.appendedBatchOffset.get
178: }
179: 
180: /**
181:  * Group contains the following metadata:
182:  *
183:  *  Membership metadata:
184:  *  1. Members registered in this group
185:  *  2. Current protocol assigned to the group (e.g. partition assignment strategy for consumers)
186:  *  3. Protocol metadata associated with group members
187:  *
188:  *  State metadata:
189:  *  1. group state
190:  *  2. generation id
191:  *  3. leader id
192:  */
193: @nonthreadsafe
194: private[group] class GroupMetadata(val groupId: String, initialState: GroupState, time: Time) extends Logging {
195:   type JoinCallback = JoinGroupResult => Unit
196: 
197:   private[group] val lock = new ReentrantLock
198: 
199:   private var state: GroupState = initialState
200:   var currentStateTimestamp: Option[Long] = Some(time.milliseconds())
201:   var protocolType: Option[String] = None
202:   var protocolName: Option[String] = None
203:   var generationId = 0
204:   private var leaderId: Option[String] = None
205: 
206:   private val members = new mutable.HashMap[String, MemberMetadata]
207:   // Static membership mapping [key: group.instance.id, value: member.id]
208:   private val staticMembers = new mutable.HashMap[String, String]
209:   private val pendingMembers = new mutable.HashSet[String]
210:   private var numMembersAwaitingJoin = 0
211:   private val supportedProtocols = new mutable.HashMap[String, Integer]().withDefaultValue(0)
212:   private val offsets = new mutable.HashMap[TopicPartition, CommitRecordMetadataAndOffset]
213:   private val pendingOffsetCommits = new mutable.HashMap[TopicPartition, OffsetAndMetadata]
214:   private val pendingTransactionalOffsetCommits = new mutable.HashMap[Long, mutable.Map[TopicPartition, CommitRecordMetadataAndOffset]]()
215:   private var receivedTransactionalOffsetCommits = false
216:   private var receivedConsumerOffsetCommits = false
217:   private val pendingSyncMembers = new mutable.HashSet[String]
218: 
219:   // When protocolType == `consumer`, a set of subscribed topics is maintained. The set is
220:   // computed when a new generation is created or when the group is restored from the log.
221:   private var subscribedTopics: Option[Set[String]] = None
222: 
223:   var newMemberAdded: Boolean = false
224: 
225:   def inLock[T](fun: => T): T = CoreUtils.inLock(lock)(fun)
226: 
227:   def is(groupState: GroupState): Boolean = state == groupState
228:   def has(memberId: String): Boolean = members.contains(memberId)
229:   def get(memberId: String): MemberMetadata = members(memberId)
230:   def size: Int = members.size
231: 
232:   def isLeader(memberId: String): Boolean = leaderId.contains(memberId)
233:   def leaderOrNull: String = leaderId.orNull
234:   def currentStateTimestampOrDefault: Long = currentStateTimestamp.getOrElse(-1)
235: 
236:   def isConsumerGroup: Boolean = protocolType.contains(ConsumerProtocol.PROTOCOL_TYPE)
237: 
238:   def add(member: MemberMetadata, callback: JoinCallback = null): Unit = {
239:     member.groupInstanceId.foreach { instanceId =>
240:       if (staticMembers.contains(instanceId))
241:         throw new IllegalStateException(s"Static member with groupInstanceId=$instanceId " +
242:           s"cannot be added to group $groupId since it is already a member")
243:       staticMembers.put(instanceId, member.memberId)
244:     }
245: 
246:     if (members.isEmpty)
247:       this.protocolType = Some(member.protocolType)
248: 
249:     assert(this.protocolType.orNull == member.protocolType)
250:     assert(supportsProtocols(member.protocolType, MemberMetadata.plainProtocolSet(member.supportedProtocols)))
251: 
252:     if (leaderId.isEmpty)
253:       leaderId = Some(member.memberId)
254: 
255:     members.put(member.memberId, member)
256:     incSupportedProtocols(member)
257:     member.awaitingJoinCallback = callback
258: 
259:     if (member.isAwaitingJoin)
260:       numMembersAwaitingJoin += 1
261: 
262:     pendingMembers.remove(member.memberId)
263:   }
264: 
265:   def remove(memberId: String): Unit = {
266:     members.remove(memberId).foreach { member =>
267:       decSupportedProtocols(member)
268:       if (member.isAwaitingJoin)
269:         numMembersAwaitingJoin -= 1
270: 
271:       member.groupInstanceId.foreach(staticMembers.remove)
272:     }
273: 
274:     if (isLeader(memberId))
275:       leaderId = members.keys.headOption
276: 
277:     pendingMembers.remove(memberId)
278:     pendingSyncMembers.remove(memberId)
279:   }
280: 
281:   /**
282:     * Check whether current leader is rejoined. If not, try to find another joined member to be
283:     * new leader. Return false if
284:     *   1. the group is currently empty (has no designated leader)
285:     *   2. no member rejoined
286:     */
287:   def maybeElectNewJoinedLeader(): Boolean = {
288:     leaderId.exists { currentLeaderId =>
289:       val currentLeader = get(currentLeaderId)
290:       if (!currentLeader.isAwaitingJoin) {
291:         members.find(_._2.isAwaitingJoin) match {
292:           case Some((anyJoinedMemberId, anyJoinedMember)) =>
293:             leaderId = Option(anyJoinedMemberId)
294:             info(s"Group leader [member.id: ${currentLeader.memberId}, " +
295:               s"group.instance.id: ${currentLeader.groupInstanceId}] failed to join " +
296:               s"before rebalance timeout, while new leader $anyJoinedMember was elected.")
297:             true
298: 
299:           case None =>
300:             info(s"Group leader [member.id: ${currentLeader.memberId}, " +
301:               s"group.instance.id: ${currentLeader.groupInstanceId}] failed to join " +
302:               s"before rebalance timeout, and the group couldn't proceed to next generation" +
303:               s"because no member joined.")
304:             false
305:         }
306:       } else {
307:         true
308:       }
309:     }
310:   }
311: 
312:   /**
313:     * [For static members only]: Replace the old member id with the new one,
314:     * keep everything else unchanged and return the updated member.
315:     */
316:   def replaceStaticMember(
317:     groupInstanceId: String,
318:     oldMemberId: String,
319:     newMemberId: String
320:   ): MemberMetadata = {
321:     val memberMetadata = members.remove(oldMemberId)
322:       .getOrElse(throw new IllegalArgumentException(s"Cannot replace non-existing member id $oldMemberId"))
323: 
324:     // Fence potential duplicate member immediately if someone awaits join/sync callback.
325:     maybeInvokeJoinCallback(memberMetadata, JoinGroupResult(oldMemberId, Errors.FENCED_INSTANCE_ID))
326:     maybeInvokeSyncCallback(memberMetadata, SyncGroupResult(Errors.FENCED_INSTANCE_ID))
327: 
328:     memberMetadata.memberId = newMemberId
329:     members.put(newMemberId, memberMetadata)
330: 
331:     if (isLeader(oldMemberId)) {
332:       leaderId = Some(newMemberId)
333:     }
334: 
335:     staticMembers.put(groupInstanceId, newMemberId)
336:     memberMetadata
337:   }
338: 
339:   def isPendingMember(memberId: String): Boolean = pendingMembers.contains(memberId)
340: 
341:   def addPendingMember(memberId: String): Boolean = {
342:     if (has(memberId)) {
343:       throw new IllegalStateException(s"Attempt to add pending member $memberId which is already " +
344:         s"a stable member of the group")
345:     }
346:     pendingMembers.add(memberId)
347:   }
348: 
349:   def addPendingSyncMember(memberId: String): Boolean = {
350:     if (!has(memberId)) {
351:       throw new IllegalStateException(s"Attempt to add a pending sync for member $memberId which " +
352:         "is not a member of the group")
353:     }
354:     pendingSyncMembers.add(memberId)
355:   }
356: 
357:   def removePendingSyncMember(memberId: String): Boolean = {
358:     if (!has(memberId)) {
359:       throw new IllegalStateException(s"Attempt to remove a pending sync for member $memberId which " +
360:         "is not a member of the group")
361:     }
362:     pendingSyncMembers.remove(memberId)
363:   }
364: 
365:   def hasReceivedSyncFromAllMembers: Boolean = {
366:     pendingSyncMembers.isEmpty
367:   }
368: 
369:   def allPendingSyncMembers: Set[String] = {
370:     pendingSyncMembers.toSet
371:   }
372: 
373:   def clearPendingSyncMembers(): Unit = {
374:     pendingSyncMembers.clear()
375:   }
376: 
377:   def hasStaticMember(groupInstanceId: String): Boolean = {
378:     staticMembers.contains(groupInstanceId)
379:   }
380: 
381:   def currentStaticMemberId(groupInstanceId: String): Option[String] = {
382:     staticMembers.get(groupInstanceId)
383:   }
384: 
385:   def currentState: GroupState = state
386: 
387:   def notYetRejoinedMembers: Map[String, MemberMetadata] = members.filter(!_._2.isAwaitingJoin).toMap
388: 
389:   def hasAllMembersJoined: Boolean = members.size == numMembersAwaitingJoin && pendingMembers.isEmpty
390: 
391:   def allMembers: collection.Set[String] = members.keySet
392: 
393:   def allStaticMembers: collection.Set[String] = staticMembers.keySet
394: 
395:   // For testing only.
396:   private[group] def allDynamicMembers: Set[String] = {
397:     val dynamicMemberSet = new mutable.HashSet[String]
398:     allMembers.foreach(memberId => dynamicMemberSet.add(memberId))
399:     staticMembers.values.foreach(memberId => dynamicMemberSet.remove(memberId))
400:     dynamicMemberSet.toSet

406:   @Test
407:   def testLastJoiningMembersAreKickedOutWhenReJoiningGroupWithMaxSize(): Unit = {
408:     val nbMembers = GroupMaxSize + 2
409:     val group = new GroupMetadata(groupId, Stable, new MockTime())
410:     val memberIds = 1.to(nbMembers).map(_ => group.generateMemberId(ClientId, None))
411: 
412:     memberIds.foreach { memberId =>
413:       group.add(new MemberMetadata(memberId, None, ClientId, ClientHost,
414:         DefaultRebalanceTimeout, GroupMaxSessionTimeout, protocolType, protocols))
415:     }
416:     groupCoordinator.groupManager.addGroup(group)
417: 
418:     groupCoordinator.prepareRebalance(group, "")
419: 
420:     val futures = memberIds.map { memberId =>
421:       EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
422:       sendJoinGroup(groupId, memberId, protocolType, protocols,
423:         None, GroupMaxSessionTimeout, DefaultRebalanceTimeout)
424:     }
425: 
426:     // advance clock by GroupInitialRebalanceDelay to complete first InitialDelayedJoin
427:     timer.advanceClock(DefaultRebalanceTimeout + 1)
428: 
429:     // Awaiting results
430:     val errors = futures.map(await(_, DefaultRebalanceTimeout + 1).error)
431: 
432:     assertEquals(Set(Errors.NONE), errors.take(GroupMaxSize).toSet)
433:     assertEquals(Set(Errors.GROUP_MAX_SIZE_REACHED), errors.drop(GroupMaxSize).toSet)
434: 
435:     memberIds.drop(GroupMaxSize).foreach { memberId =>
436:       assertFalse(group.has(memberId))
437:     }
438:   }

544:   @Test
545:   def testNewMemberJoinExpiration(): Unit = {
546:     // This tests new member expiration during a protracted rebalance. We first create a
547:     // group with one member which uses a large value for session timeout and rebalance timeout.
548:     // We then join with one new member and let the rebalance hang while we await the first member.
549:     // The new member join timeout expires and its JoinGroup request is failed.
550: 
551:     val sessionTimeout = GroupCoordinator.NewMemberJoinTimeoutMs + 5000
552:     val rebalanceTimeout = GroupCoordinator.NewMemberJoinTimeoutMs * 2
553: 
554:     val firstJoinResult = dynamicJoinGroup(groupId, JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID, protocolType, protocols,
555:       sessionTimeout, rebalanceTimeout)
556:     val firstMemberId = firstJoinResult.memberId
557:     assertEquals(firstMemberId, firstJoinResult.leaderId)
558:     assertEquals(Errors.NONE, firstJoinResult.error)
559: 
560:     val groupOpt = groupCoordinator.groupManager.getGroup(groupId)
561:     assertTrue(groupOpt.isDefined)
562:     val group = groupOpt.get
563:     assertEquals(0, group.allMemberMetadata.count(_.isNew))
564: 
565:     EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
566:     val responseFuture = sendJoinGroup(groupId, JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID, protocolType, protocols, None, sessionTimeout, rebalanceTimeout)
567:     assertFalse(responseFuture.isCompleted)
568: 
569:     assertEquals(2, group.allMembers.size)
570:     assertEquals(1, group.allMemberMetadata.count(_.isNew))
571: 
572:     val newMember = group.allMemberMetadata.find(_.isNew).get
573:     assertNotEquals(firstMemberId, newMember.memberId)
574: 
575:     timer.advanceClock(GroupCoordinator.NewMemberJoinTimeoutMs + 1)
576:     assertTrue(responseFuture.isCompleted)
577: 
578:     val response = Await.result(responseFuture, Duration(0, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS))
579:     assertEquals(Errors.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID, response.error)
580:     assertEquals(1, group.allMembers.size)
581:     assertEquals(0, group.allMemberMetadata.count(_.isNew))
582:     assertEquals(firstMemberId, group.allMembers.head)
583:   }

794:   @Test
795:   def staticMemberFenceDuplicateSyncingFollowerAfterMemberIdChanged(): Unit = {
796:     val rebalanceResult = staticMembersJoinAndRebalance(leaderInstanceId, followerInstanceId)
797: 
798:     // Known leader rejoins will trigger rebalance.
799:     val leaderJoinGroupFuture =
800:       sendJoinGroup(groupId, rebalanceResult.leaderId, protocolType, protocols, groupInstanceId = Some(leaderInstanceId))
801:     timer.advanceClock(1)
802:     assertTrue(getGroup(groupId).is(PreparingRebalance))
803: 
804:     EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
805:     timer.advanceClock(1)
806:     // Old follower rejoins group will match current member.id.
807:     val oldFollowerJoinGroupFuture =
808:       sendJoinGroup(groupId, rebalanceResult.followerId, protocolType, protocols, groupInstanceId = Some(followerInstanceId))
809: 
810:     timer.advanceClock(1)
811:     val leaderJoinGroupResult = Await.result(leaderJoinGroupFuture, Duration(1, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS))
812:     checkJoinGroupResult(leaderJoinGroupResult,
813:       Errors.NONE,
814:       rebalanceResult.generation + 1,
815:       Set(leaderInstanceId, followerInstanceId),
816:       CompletingRebalance,
817:       Some(protocolType))
818:     assertEquals(rebalanceResult.leaderId, leaderJoinGroupResult.memberId)
819:     assertEquals(rebalanceResult.leaderId, leaderJoinGroupResult.leaderId)
820: 
821:     // Old follower shall be getting a successful join group response.
822:     val oldFollowerJoinGroupResult = Await.result(oldFollowerJoinGroupFuture, Duration(1, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS))
823:     checkJoinGroupResult(oldFollowerJoinGroupResult,
824:       Errors.NONE,
825:       rebalanceResult.generation + 1,
826:       Set.empty,
827:       CompletingRebalance,
828:       Some(protocolType),
829:       expectedLeaderId = leaderJoinGroupResult.memberId)
830:     assertEquals(rebalanceResult.followerId, oldFollowerJoinGroupResult.memberId)
831:     assertEquals(rebalanceResult.leaderId, oldFollowerJoinGroupResult.leaderId)
832:     assertTrue(getGroup(groupId).is(CompletingRebalance))
833: 
834:     // Duplicate follower joins group with unknown member id will trigger member.id replacement,
835:     // and will also trigger a rebalance under CompletingRebalance state; the old follower sync callback
836:     // will return fenced exception while broker replaces the member identity with the duplicate follower joins.
837:     EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
838:     val oldFollowerSyncGroupFuture = sendSyncGroupFollower(groupId, oldFollowerJoinGroupResult.generationId,
839:       oldFollowerJoinGroupResult.memberId, Some(protocolType), Some(protocolName), Some(followerInstanceId))
840: 
841:     EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
842:     val duplicateFollowerJoinFuture =
843:       sendJoinGroup(groupId, JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID, protocolType, protocols, groupInstanceId = Some(followerInstanceId))
844:     timer.advanceClock(1)
845: 
846:     val oldFollowerSyncGroupResult = Await.result(oldFollowerSyncGroupFuture, Duration(1, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS))
847:     assertEquals(Errors.FENCED_INSTANCE_ID, oldFollowerSyncGroupResult.error)
848:     assertTrue(getGroup(groupId).is(PreparingRebalance))
849: 
850:     timer.advanceClock(GroupInitialRebalanceDelay + 1)
851:     timer.advanceClock(DefaultRebalanceTimeout + 1)
852: 
853:     val duplicateFollowerJoinGroupResult = Await.result(duplicateFollowerJoinFuture, Duration(1, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS))
854:     checkJoinGroupResult(duplicateFollowerJoinGroupResult,
855:       Errors.NONE,
856:       rebalanceResult.generation + 2,
857:       Set(followerInstanceId),   // this follower will become the new leader, and hence it would have the member list
858:       CompletingRebalance,
859:       Some(protocolType),
860:       expectedLeaderId = duplicateFollowerJoinGroupResult.memberId)
861:     assertTrue(getGroup(groupId).is(CompletingRebalance))
862:   }

1124:

1126:     val leaderRejoinGroupFuture = sendJoinGroup(groupId, rebalanceResult.leaderId, protocolType,

1128:     // Rebalance complete immediately after follower rejoin.

1130:     val followerRejoinWithFuture = sendJoinGroup(groupId, rebalanceResult.followerId, protocolType,
1131:       protocolSuperset, Some(followerInstanceId))
1132:

1134: 
1135:     // Leader should get the same assignment as last round.
1136:     checkJoinGroupResult(await(leaderRejoinGroupFuture, 1),
1137:       Errors.NONE,

1139:       Set(leaderInstanceId, followerInstanceId),

1146:       Errors.NONE,

1148:       Set.empty,

1151:       rebalanceResult.leaderId,
1152:       rebalanceResult.followerId)

1155:     // The follower protocol changed from protocolSuperset to general protocols.
1156:     val followerRejoinWithProtocolChangeFuture = sendJoinGroup(groupId, rebalanceResult.followerId,
1157:       protocolType, protocols, Some(followerInstanceId))
1158:     // The group will transit to PreparingRebalance due to protocol change from follower.
1159:     assertTrue(getGroup(groupId).is(PreparingRebalance))

1162:     checkJoinGroupResult(await(followerRejoinWithProtocolChangeFuture, 1),

1165:       Set(followerInstanceId),

1363:   def groupStuckInRebalanceTimeoutDueToNonjoinedStaticMember(): Unit = {
1364:     val longSessionTimeout = DefaultSessionTimeout * 2
1365:     val rebalanceResult = staticMembersJoinAndRebalance(leaderInstanceId, followerInstanceId, sessionTimeout = longSessionTimeout)
1366: 
1367:     val dynamicJoinFuture = sendJoinGroup(groupId, JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID, protocolType, protocolSuperset, sessionTimeout = longSessionTimeout)
1368:     timer.advanceClock(DefaultRebalanceTimeout + 1)
1369: 
1370:     val dynamicJoinResult = await(dynamicJoinFuture, 100)
1371:     // The new dynamic member has been elected as leader
1372:     assertEquals(dynamicJoinResult.leaderId, dynamicJoinResult.memberId)
1373:     assertEquals(Errors.NONE, dynamicJoinResult.error)
1374:     assertEquals(3, dynamicJoinResult.members.size)
1375:     assertEquals(2, dynamicJoinResult.generationId)
1376:     assertGroupState(groupState = CompletingRebalance)
1377: 
1378:     assertEquals(Set(rebalanceResult.leaderId, rebalanceResult.followerId,
1379:       dynamicJoinResult.memberId), getGroup(groupId).allMembers)
1380:     assertEquals(Set(leaderInstanceId, followerInstanceId),
1381:       getGroup(groupId).allStaticMembers)
1382:     assertEquals(Set(dynamicJoinResult.memberId), getGroup(groupId).allDynamicMembers)
1383: 
1384:     // Send a special leave group request from static follower, moving group towards PreparingRebalance
1385:     EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
1386:     val followerLeaveGroupResults = singleLeaveGroup(groupId, rebalanceResult.followerId)
1387:     verifyLeaveGroupResult(followerLeaveGroupResults)
1388:     assertGroupState(groupState = PreparingRebalance)
1389: 
1390:     timer.advanceClock(DefaultRebalanceTimeout + 1)
1391:     // Only static leader is maintained, and group is stuck at PreparingRebalance stage
1392:     assertTrue(getGroup(groupId).allDynamicMembers.isEmpty)
1393:     assertEquals(Set(rebalanceResult.leaderId), getGroup(groupId).allMembers)
1394:     assertTrue(getGroup(groupId).allDynamicMembers.isEmpty)
1395:     assertEquals(2, getGroup(groupId).generationId)
1396:     assertGroupState(groupState = PreparingRebalance)
1397:   }

1575:   private class RebalanceResult(val generation: Int,
1576:                                 val leaderId: String,
1577:                                 val leaderAssignment: Array[Byte],
1578:                                 val followerId: String,
1579:                                 val followerAssignment: Array[Byte])
1580:   /**
1581:     * Generate static member rebalance results, including:
1582:     *   - generation
1583:     *   - leader id
1584:     *   - leader assignment
1585:     *   - follower id
1586:     *   - follower assignment
1587:     */
1588:   private def staticMembersJoinAndRebalance(leaderInstanceId: String,
1589:                                             followerInstanceId: String,
1590:                                             sessionTimeout: Int = DefaultSessionTimeout): RebalanceResult = {
1591:     EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
1592:     val leaderResponseFuture = sendJoinGroup(groupId, JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID, protocolType,
1593:       protocolSuperset, Some(leaderInstanceId), sessionTimeout)
1594: 
1595:     EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
1596:     val followerResponseFuture = sendJoinGroup(groupId, JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID, protocolType,
1597:       protocolSuperset, Some(followerInstanceId), sessionTimeout)
1598:     // The goal for two timer advance is to let first group initial join complete and set newMemberAdded flag to false. Next advance is
1599:     // to trigger the rebalance as needed for follower delayed join. One large time advance won't help because we could only populate one
1600:     // delayed join from purgatory and the new delayed op is created at that time and never be triggered.

1633:   private def checkJoinGroupResult(joinGroupResult: JoinGroupResult,
1634:                                    expectedError: Errors,
1635:                                    expectedGeneration: Int,
1636:                                    expectedGroupInstanceIds: Set[String],
1637:                                    expectedGroupState: GroupState,
1638:                                    expectedProtocolType: Option[String],
1639:                                    expectedLeaderId: String = JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID,
1640:                                    expectedMemberId: String = JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID): Unit = {
1641:     assertEquals(expectedError, joinGroupResult.error)
1642:     assertEquals(expectedGeneration, joinGroupResult.generationId)
1643:     assertEquals(expectedGroupInstanceIds.size, joinGroupResult.members.size)
1644:     val resultedGroupInstanceIds = joinGroupResult.members.map(member => member.groupInstanceId).toSet
1645:     assertEquals(expectedGroupInstanceIds, resultedGroupInstanceIds)
1646:     assertGroupState(groupState = expectedGroupState)
1647:     assertEquals(expectedProtocolType, joinGroupResult.protocolType)
1648: 
1649:     if (!expectedLeaderId.equals(JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID)) {
1650:       assertEquals(expectedLeaderId, joinGroupResult.leaderId)

2093:   /**
2094:     * Test if the following scenario completes a rebalance correctly: A new member starts a JoinGroup request with
2095:     * an UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID, attempting to join a stable group. But never initiates the second JoinGroup request with
2096:     * the provided member ID and times out. The test checks if original member remains the sole member in this group,
2097:     * which should remain stable throughout this test.
2098:     */
2099:   @Test
2100:   def testSecondMemberPartiallyJoinAndTimeout(): Unit = {
2101:     val firstJoinResult = dynamicJoinGroup(groupId, JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID, protocolType, protocols)
2102:     val firstMemberId = firstJoinResult.memberId
2103:     val firstGenerationId = firstJoinResult.generationId
2104:     assertEquals(firstMemberId, firstJoinResult.leaderId)
2105:     assertEquals(Errors.NONE, firstJoinResult.error)
2106: 
2107:     // Starting sync group leader
2108:     EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
2109:     val firstSyncResult = syncGroupLeader(groupId, firstGenerationId, firstMemberId, Map(firstMemberId -> Array[Byte]()))
2110:     assertEquals(Errors.NONE, firstSyncResult.error)
2111:     timer.advanceClock(100)
2112:     assertEquals(Set(firstMemberId), groupCoordinator.groupManager.getGroup(groupId).get.allMembers)
2113:     assertEquals(groupCoordinator.groupManager.getGroup(groupId).get.allMembers,
2114:       groupCoordinator.groupManager.getGroup(groupId).get.allDynamicMembers)
2115:     assertEquals(0, groupCoordinator.groupManager.getGroup(groupId).get.numPending)
2116:     val group = groupCoordinator.groupManager.getGroup(groupId).get
2117: 
2118:     // ensure the group is stable before a new member initiates join request
2119:     assertEquals(Stable, group.currentState)
2120: 
2121:     // new member initiates join group
2122:     EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
2123:     val secondJoinResult = joinGroupPartial(groupId, JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID, protocolType, protocols)
2124:     assertEquals(Errors.MEMBER_ID_REQUIRED, secondJoinResult.error)
2125:     assertEquals(1, group.numPending)
2126:     assertEquals(Stable, group.currentState)
2127: 
2128:     EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
2129:     EasyMock.expect(replicaManager.getMagic(EasyMock.anyObject())).andReturn(Some(RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V1)).anyTimes()
2130:     EasyMock.replay(replicaManager)
2131: 
2132:     // advance clock to timeout the pending member
2133:     assertEquals(Set(firstMemberId), group.allMembers)
2134:     assertEquals(1, group.numPending)
2135:     timer.advanceClock(300)
2136: 
2137:     // original (firstMember) member sends heartbeats to prevent session timeouts.
2138:     EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
2139:     val heartbeatResult = heartbeat(groupId, firstMemberId, 1)
2140:     assertEquals(Errors.NONE, heartbeatResult)
2141: 
2142:     // timeout the pending member
2143:     timer.advanceClock(300)
2144: 
2145:     // at this point the second member should have been removed from pending list (session timeout),
2146:     // and the group should be in Stable state with only the first member in it.
2147:     assertEquals(Set(firstMemberId), group.allMembers)
2148:     assertEquals(0, group.numPending)
2149:     assertEquals(Stable, group.currentState)
2150:     assertTrue(group.has(firstMemberId))
2151:   }
2152: 
2153:   /**
2154:     * Create a group with two members in Stable state. Create a third pending member by completing it's first JoinGroup
2155:     * request without a member id.
2156:     */
2157:   private def setupGroupWithPendingMember(): JoinGroupResult = {
2158:     // add the first member
2159:     val joinResult1 = dynamicJoinGroup(groupId, JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID, protocolType, protocols)
2160:     assertGroupState(groupState = CompletingRebalance)
2161: 
2162:     // now the group is stable, with the one member that joined above
2163:     EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
2164:     val firstSyncResult = syncGroupLeader(groupId, joinResult1.generationId, joinResult1.memberId, Map(joinResult1.memberId -> Array[Byte]()))

2232:     // completion on heartbeat expiration (since we are in PendingRebalance stage).

2259:     expectedError: Errors

2270:   private def joinWithNMembers(nbMembers: Int): Seq[JoinGroupResult] = {
2271:     val requiredKnownMemberId = true
2272: 
2273:     // First JoinRequests
2274:     var futures = 1.to(nbMembers).map { _ =>
2275:       EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
2276:       sendJoinGroup(groupId, JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID, protocolType, protocols,
2277:         None, DefaultSessionTimeout, DefaultRebalanceTimeout, requiredKnownMemberId)
2278:     }
2279: 
2280:     // Get back the assigned member ids
2281:     val memberIds = futures.map(await(_, 1).memberId)
2282: 
2283:     // Second JoinRequests
2284:     futures = memberIds.map { memberId =>
2285:       EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
2286:       sendJoinGroup(groupId, memberId, protocolType, protocols,
2287:         None, DefaultSessionTimeout, DefaultRebalanceTimeout, requiredKnownMemberId)
2288:     }
2289: 
2290:     timer.advanceClock(GroupInitialRebalanceDelay + 1)
2291:     timer.advanceClock(DefaultRebalanceTimeout + 1)
2292: 
2293:     futures.map(await(_, 1))
2294:   }

2296:   @Test
2297:   def testRebalanceTimesOutWhenSyncRequestIsNotReceived(): Unit = {
2298:     // This test case ensure that the DelayedSync does kick out all members
2299:     // if they don't sent a sync request before the rebalance timeout. The
2300:     // group is in the Stable state in this case.
2301:     val results = joinWithNMembers(nbMembers = 3)
2302:     assertEquals(Set(Errors.NONE), results.map(_.error).toSet)
2303: 
2304:     // Advance time
2305:     timer.advanceClock(DefaultRebalanceTimeout / 2)
2306: 
2307:     // Heartbeats to ensure that heartbeating does not interfere with the
2308:     // delayed sync operation.
2309:     results.foreach { joinGroupResult =>
2310:       verifyHeartbeat(joinGroupResult, Errors.NONE)
2311:     }
2312: 
2313:     // Advance part the rebalance timeout to trigger the delayed operation.
2314:     EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
2315:     EasyMock.expect(replicaManager.getMagic(EasyMock.anyObject()))
2316:       .andReturn(Some(RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V1))
2317:       .anyTimes()
2318:     EasyMock.replay(replicaManager)
2319: 
2320:     timer.advanceClock(DefaultRebalanceTimeout / 2 + 1)
2321: 
2322:     // Heartbeats fail because none of the members have sent the sync request
2323:     results.foreach { joinGroupResult =>
2324:       verifyHeartbeat(joinGroupResult, Errors.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID)
2325:     }
2326:   }

2328:   @Test
2329:   def testRebalanceTimesOutWhenSyncRequestIsNotReceivedFromFollowers(): Unit = {
2330:     // This test case ensure that the DelayedSync does kick out the followers
2331:     // if they don't sent a sync request before the rebalance timeout. The
2332:     // group is in the Stable state in this case.
2333:     val results = joinWithNMembers(nbMembers = 3)
2334:     assertEquals(Set(Errors.NONE), results.map(_.error).toSet)
2335: 
2336:     // Advance time
2337:     timer.advanceClock(DefaultRebalanceTimeout / 2)
2338: 
2339:     // Heartbeats to ensure that heartbeating does not interfere with the
2340:     // delayed sync operation.
2341:     results.foreach { joinGroupResult =>
2342:       verifyHeartbeat(joinGroupResult, Errors.NONE)
2343:     }
2344: 
2345:     // Leader sends Sync
2346:     EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
2347:     val assignments = results.map(result => result.memberId -> Array.empty[Byte]).toMap
2348:     val leaderResult = sendSyncGroupLeader(groupId, results.head.generationId, results.head.memberId,
2349:       Some(protocolType), Some(protocolName), None, assignments)
2350: 
2351:     assertEquals(Errors.NONE, await(leaderResult, 1).error)
2352: 
2353:     // Leader should be able to heartbeart
2354:     verifyHeartbeat(results.head, Errors.NONE)
2355: 
2356:     // Advance part the rebalance timeout to trigger the delayed operation.
2357:     timer.advanceClock(DefaultRebalanceTimeout / 2 + 1)
2358: 
2359:     // Leader should be able to heartbeart
2360:     verifyHeartbeat(results.head, Errors.REBALANCE_IN_PROGRESS)
2361: 
2362:     // Followers should have been removed.
2363:     results.tail.foreach { joinGroupResult =>
2364:       verifyHeartbeat(joinGroupResult, Errors.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID)
2365:     }
2366:   }

2370:     // This test case ensure that the DelayedSync does kick out the leader
2371:     // if it does not sent a sync request before the rebalance timeout. The
2372:     // group is in the CompletingRebalance state in this case.

2383:     }

2385:     // Followers send Sync
2386:     EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
2387:     val followerResults = results.tail.map { joinGroupResult =>

2397:     assertEquals(Set(Errors.REBALANCE_IN_PROGRESS), followerErrors.toSet)

2408:   @Test
2409:   def testRebalanceDoesNotTimeOutWhenAllSyncAreReceived(): Unit = {
2410:     // This test case ensure that the DelayedSync does not kick any
2411:     // members out when they have all sent their sync requests.
2412:     val results = joinWithNMembers(nbMembers = 3)
2413:     assertEquals(Set(Errors.NONE), results.map(_.error).toSet)
2414: 
2415:     // Advance time
2416:     timer.advanceClock(DefaultRebalanceTimeout / 2)
2417: 
2418:     // Heartbeats to ensure that heartbeating does not interfere with the
2419:     // delayed sync operation.
2420:     results.foreach { joinGroupResult =>
2421:       verifyHeartbeat(joinGroupResult, Errors.NONE)
2422:     }
2423: 
2424:     EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
2425:     val assignments = results.map(result => result.memberId -> Array.empty[Byte]).toMap
2426:     val leaderResult = sendSyncGroupLeader(groupId, results.head.generationId, results.head.memberId,
2427:       Some(protocolType), Some(protocolName), None, assignments)
2428: 
2429:     assertEquals(Errors.NONE, await(leaderResult, 1).error)
2430: 
2431:     // Followers send Sync
2432:     EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
2433:     val followerResults = results.tail.map { joinGroupResult =>
2434:       EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
2435:       sendSyncGroupFollower(groupId, joinGroupResult.generationId, joinGroupResult.memberId,
2436:         Some(protocolType), Some(protocolName), None)
2437:     }
2438: 
2439:     val followerErrors = followerResults.map(await(_, 1).error)
2440:     assertEquals(Set(Errors.NONE), followerErrors.toSet)
2441: 
2442:     // Advance past the rebalance timeout to expire the Sync timout. All
2443:     // members should remain and the group should not rebalance.
2444:     timer.advanceClock(DefaultRebalanceTimeout / 2 + 1)
2445: 
2446:     // Followers should be able to heartbeat.
2447:     results.foreach { joinGroupResult =>
2448:       verifyHeartbeat(joinGroupResult, Errors.NONE)
2449:     }
2450: 
2451:     // Advance a bit more.
2452:     timer.advanceClock(DefaultRebalanceTimeout / 2)
2453: 
2454:     // Followers should be able to heartbeat.
2455:     results.foreach { joinGroupResult =>
2456:       verifyHeartbeat(joinGroupResult, Errors.NONE)
2457:     }
2458:   }

2877:     // One producer, two groups located on separate offsets topic partitions.
2878:     // Both group have pending offset commits.
2879:     // Marker for only one partition is received. That commit should be materialized while the other should not.
2880: 
2881:     val partitions = List(new TopicPartition("topic1", 0), new TopicPartition("topic2", 0))
2882:     val offsets = List(offsetAndMetadata(10), offsetAndMetadata(15))
2883:     val producerId = 1000L
2884:     val producerEpoch: Short = 3
2885: 
2886:     val groupIds = List(groupId, otherGroupId)
2887:     val offsetTopicPartitions = List(new TopicPartition(Topic.GROUP_METADATA_TOPIC_NAME, groupCoordinator.partitionFor(groupId)),
2888:       new TopicPartition(Topic.GROUP_METADATA_TOPIC_NAME, groupCoordinator.partitionFor(otherGroupId)))
2889: 
2890:     groupCoordinator.groupManager.addPartitionOwnership(offsetTopicPartitions(1).partition)
2891:     val errors = mutable.ArrayBuffer[Errors]()
2892:     val partitionData = mutable.ArrayBuffer[scala.collection.Map[TopicPartition, OffsetFetchResponse.PartitionData]]()
2893: 
2894:     val commitOffsetResults = mutable.ArrayBuffer[CommitOffsetCallbackParams]()
2895: 
2896:     // Ensure that the two groups map to different partitions.
2897:     assertNotEquals(offsetTopicPartitions(0), offsetTopicPartitions(1))
2898: 
2899:     commitOffsetResults.append(commitTransactionalOffsets(groupId, producerId, producerEpoch, Map(partitions(0) -> offsets(0))))
2900:     assertEquals(Errors.NONE, commitOffsetResults(0)(partitions(0)))
2901:     commitOffsetResults.append(commitTransactionalOffsets(otherGroupId, producerId, producerEpoch, Map(partitions(1) -> offsets(1))))
2902:     assertEquals(Errors.NONE, commitOffsetResults(1)(partitions(1)))
2903: 
2904:     // We got a commit for only one __consumer_offsets partition. We should only materialize it's group offsets.
2905:     handleTxnCompletion(producerId, List(offsetTopicPartitions(0)), TransactionResult.COMMIT)
2906:     groupCoordinator.handleFetchOffsets(groupIds(0), requireStable, Some(partitions)) match {
2907:       case (error, partData) =>
2908:         errors.append(error)
2909:         partitionData.append(partData)
2910:       case _ =>
2911:     }
2912: 
2913:     groupCoordinator.handleFetchOffsets(groupIds(1), requireStable, Some(partitions)) match {
2914:       case (error, partData) =>
2915:         errors.append(error)
2916:         partitionData.append(partData)
2917:       case _ =>
2918:     }
2919: 
2920:     assertEquals(2, errors.size)
2921:     assertEquals(Errors.NONE, errors(0))
2922:     assertEquals(Errors.NONE, errors(1))
2923: 
2924:     // Exactly one offset commit should have been materialized.
2925:     assertEquals(Some(offsets(0).offset), partitionData(0).get(partitions(0)).map(_.offset))
2926:     assertEquals(Some(OffsetFetchResponse.INVALID_OFFSET), partitionData(0).get(partitions(1)).map(_.offset))
2927:     assertEquals(Some(OffsetFetchResponse.INVALID_OFFSET), partitionData(1).get(partitions(0)).map(_.offset))
2928:     assertEquals(Some(OffsetFetchResponse.INVALID_OFFSET), partitionData(1).get(partitions(1)).map(_.offset))
2929: 
2930:     // Now we receive the other marker.
2931:     handleTxnCompletion(producerId, List(offsetTopicPartitions(1)), TransactionResult.COMMIT)
2932:     errors.clear()
2933:     partitionData.clear()
2934:     groupCoordinator.handleFetchOffsets(groupIds(0), requireStable, Some(partitions)) match {
2935:       case (error, partData) =>
2936:         errors.append(error)
2937:         partitionData.append(partData)
2938:       case _ =>
2939:     }
2940: 
2941:      groupCoordinator.handleFetchOffsets(groupIds(1), requireStable, Some(partitions)) match {
2942:       case (error, partData) =>
2943:         errors.append(error)
2944:         partitionData.append(partData)
2945:       case _ =>
2946:     }
2947:     // Two offsets should have been materialized
2948:     assertEquals(Some(offsets(0).offset), partitionData(0).get(partitions(0)).map(_.offset))
2949:     assertEquals(Some(OffsetFetchResponse.INVALID_OFFSET), partitionData(0).get(partitions(1)).map(_.offset))
2950:     assertEquals(Some(OffsetFetchResponse.INVALID_OFFSET), partitionData(1).get(partitions(0)).map(_.offset))
2951:     assertEquals(Some(offsets(1).offset), partitionData(1).get(partitions(1)).map(_.offset))

3214:

3419:   @Test
3420:   def testListGroupsWithStates(): Unit = {
3421:     val allStates = Set(PreparingRebalance, CompletingRebalance, Stable, Dead, Empty).map(s => s.toString)
3422:     val memberId = JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID
3423: 
3424:     // Member joins the group
3425:     val joinGroupResult = dynamicJoinGroup(groupId, memberId, protocolType, protocols)
3426:     val assignedMemberId = joinGroupResult.memberId
3427:     val generationId = joinGroupResult.generationId
3428:     assertEquals(Errors.NONE, joinGroupResult.error)
3429: 
3430:     // The group should be in CompletingRebalance
3431:     val (error, groups) = groupCoordinator.handleListGroups(Set(CompletingRebalance.toString))
3432:     assertEquals(Errors.NONE, error)
3433:     assertEquals(1, groups.size)
3434:     val (error2, groups2) = groupCoordinator.handleListGroups(allStates.filterNot(s => s == CompletingRebalance.toString))
3435:     assertEquals(Errors.NONE, error2)
3436:     assertEquals(0, groups2.size)
3437: 
3438:     // Member syncs
3439:     EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
3440:     val syncGroupResult = syncGroupLeader(groupId, generationId, assignedMemberId, Map(assignedMemberId -> Array[Byte]()))
3441:     assertEquals(Errors.NONE, syncGroupResult.error)
3442: 
3443:     // The group is now stable
3444:     val (error3, groups3) = groupCoordinator.handleListGroups(Set(Stable.toString))
3445:     assertEquals(Errors.NONE, error3)
3446:     assertEquals(1, groups3.size)
3447:     val (error4, groups4) = groupCoordinator.handleListGroups(allStates.filterNot(s => s == Stable.toString))
3448:     assertEquals(Errors.NONE, error4)
3449:     assertEquals(0, groups4.size)
3450: 
3451:     // Member leaves
3452:     EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
3453:     val leaveGroupResults = singleLeaveGroup(groupId, assignedMemberId)
3454:     verifyLeaveGroupResult(leaveGroupResults)
3455: 
3456:     // The group is now empty
3457:     val (error5, groups5) = groupCoordinator.handleListGroups(Set(Empty.toString))
3458:     assertEquals(Errors.NONE, error5)
3459:     assertEquals(1, groups5.size)
3460:     val (error6, groups6) = groupCoordinator.handleListGroups(allStates.filterNot(s => s == Empty.toString))
3461:     assertEquals(Errors.NONE, error6)
3462:     assertEquals(0, groups6.size)
3463:   }

4067:   private def dynamicJoinGroup(groupId: String,
4068:                                memberId: String,
4069:                                protocolType: String,
4070:                                protocols: List[(String, Array[Byte])],
4071:                                sessionTimeout: Int = DefaultSessionTimeout,
4072:                                rebalanceTimeout: Int = DefaultRebalanceTimeout): JoinGroupResult = {
4073:     val requireKnownMemberId = true
4074:     var responseFuture = sendJoinGroup(groupId, memberId, protocolType, protocols, None, sessionTimeout, rebalanceTimeout, requireKnownMemberId)
4075: 
4076:     // Since member id is required, we need another bounce to get the successful join group result.
4077:     if (memberId == JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID && requireKnownMemberId) {
4078:       val joinGroupResult = Await.result(responseFuture, Duration(rebalanceTimeout + 100, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS))
4079:       // If some other error is triggered, return the error immediately for caller to handle.
4080:       if (joinGroupResult.error != Errors.MEMBER_ID_REQUIRED) {
4081:         return joinGroupResult
4082:       }
4083:       EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
4084:       responseFuture = sendJoinGroup(groupId, joinGroupResult.memberId, protocolType, protocols, None, sessionTimeout, rebalanceTimeout, requireKnownMemberId)
4085:     }
4086:     timer.advanceClock(GroupInitialRebalanceDelay + 1)

# Test Coverage Gap Analysis: Kafka Consumer Group Coordinator

## Summary

Analysis is based on reading the full source of `GroupCoordinator.scala` (1,681 lines) and
`GroupCoordinatorTest.scala` (~4,100 lines), plus supporting classes in `GroupMetadata.scala`.

The existing test suite provides strong coverage for the happy-path flows (join/sync/heartbeat/
leave/offset-commit cycles), static-membership fencing, max-size enforcement, and the basic
`handleDeleteOffsets` / `handleDeleteGroups` paths.  Six concrete failure modes are NOT covered:

1. Coordinator resignation while a JoinGroup rebalance is in flight (`onGroupUnloaded` during `PreparingRebalance`)
2. Loading a group that already exceeds the configured max size (`onGroupLoaded` over-capacity)
3. Standalone offset commit against a non-empty group (`generationId < 0`, non-Empty group)
4. Persistence failure during leader `SyncGroup` (`storeGroup` returns an error in `doSyncGroup`)
5. `handleDeleteOffsets` for a consumer group in `PreparingRebalance` or `CompletingRebalance`
6. `handleDeletedPartitions` — zero tests in `GroupCoordinatorTest`

---

## Failure Modes and Proposed Tests

### Failure Mode 1: Coordinator Resignation During Active Rebalance

- **Risk**: HIGH
- **Code Location**: `GroupCoordinator.scala` ~lines 1098–1122 (`onGroupUnloaded`)
- **Description**: When `onResignation` is called (partition leadership lost) while the group is
  in `PreparingRebalance`, each member with a pending `JoinGroup` callback must receive
  `NOT_COORDINATOR` so the client can rediscover the new coordinator.  The code path that drives
  this is `onGroupUnloaded`, specifically the `PreparingRebalance` branch that iterates
  `group.allMemberMetadata` and calls `group.maybeInvokeJoinCallback`.

  No test exercises `onResignation` while a `JoinGroup` future is still parked in the purgatory.
  The only related test (`testRequestHandlingWhileLoadingInProgress`) only covers *loading*, not
  *unloading*, and only for requests that arrive after the fact.

- **Proposed Test**:
  ```scala
  @Test
  def testOnGroupUnloadedDuringPreparingRebalanceReturnsNotCoordinator(): Unit = {
    // Put one member into a stable group first.
    val firstJoinResult = dynamicJoinGroup(groupId, JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID,
      protocolType, protocols)
    val firstMemberId = firstJoinResult.memberId
    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    val firstSyncResult = syncGroupLeader(groupId, firstJoinResult.generationId,
      firstMemberId, Map(firstMemberId -> Array[Byte]()))
    assertEquals(Errors.NONE, firstSyncResult.error)

    // Trigger a rebalance by having the leader re-join; this parks a Future.
    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    val rejoinFuture = sendJoinGroup(groupId, firstMemberId, protocolType, protocols)
    assertGroupState(groupState = PreparingRebalance)

    // Simulate coordinator partition resignation while the rebalance is pending.
    groupCoordinator.onResignation(groupPartitionId, Some(1))

    // The pending JoinGroup callback must have fired with NOT_COORDINATOR.
    val rejoinResult = Await.result(rejoinFuture, Duration(1, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS))
    assertEquals(Errors.NOT_COORDINATOR, rejoinResult.error)

    // Group metadata should now be Dead and removed.
    assertGroupState(groupId, groupState = Dead)
  }
  ```
- **Coverage Delta**: Exercises `onGroupUnloaded` → `PreparingRebalance` branch
  (`maybeInvokeJoinCallback` with `NOT_COORDINATOR`), `rebalancePurgatory.checkAndComplete`,
  and `removeSyncExpiration`.  Also implicitly tests that `onResignation` → `removeGroupsForPartition`
  → `onGroupUnloaded` are wired correctly.

---

### Failure Mode 2: Over-Capacity Group Forces Rebalance on Load

- **Risk**: HIGH
- **Code Location**: `GroupCoordinator.scala` ~lines 1124–1134 (`onGroupLoaded`)
- **Description**: `onGroupLoaded` contains an explicit check:

  ```scala
  if (groupIsOverCapacity(group)) {
    prepareRebalance(group, ...)
  }
  ```

  This fires when the operator reduces `group.max.session.timeout.ms` / `group.max.size` and
  a previously larger group is loaded on a new coordinator epoch.  No test constructs a group
  whose size exceeds `groupConfig.groupMaxSize` and then calls `onGroupLoaded` (via `onElection`).

- **Proposed Test**:
  ```scala
  @Test
  def testOnGroupLoadedOverCapacityTriggersRebalance(): Unit = {
    // Build a group with GroupMaxSize+1 members directly in Stable state.
    val overCapacityGroup = new GroupMetadata(groupId, Stable, new MockTime())
    val extraMemberCount = GroupMaxSize + 1
    val memberIds = (1 to extraMemberCount).map { i =>
      overCapacityGroup.generateMemberId(ClientId, None)
    }
    memberIds.foreach { memberId =>
      val member = new MemberMetadata(memberId, None, ClientId, ClientHost,
        DefaultRebalanceTimeout, DefaultSessionTimeout, protocolType, protocols)
      overCapacityGroup.add(member)
    }
    groupCoordinator.groupManager.addGroup(overCapacityGroup)
    assertTrue(overCapacityGroup.is(Stable))
    assertEquals(extraMemberCount, overCapacityGroup.size)

    // Simulate the coordinator loading the partition owning this group.
    // onGroupLoaded is the internal callback wired through scheduleLoadGroupAndOffsets.
    groupCoordinator.onElection(groupPartitionId, coordinatorEpoch = 1)
    // Give the scheduler a chance to process the load event.
    timer.advanceClock(1)

    // Group must now be in PreparingRebalance because it exceeded max size.
    assertGroupState(groupState = PreparingRebalance)
  }
  ```
- **Coverage Delta**: The only code path reaching `onGroupLoaded → groupIsOverCapacity → prepareRebalance`
  is exercised.  Also exercises the `onElection → scheduleLoadGroupAndOffsets → onGroupLoaded` chain.

---

### Failure Mode 3: Standalone Offset Commit on Non-Empty Group Returns UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID

- **Risk**: MEDIUM
- **Code Location**: `GroupCoordinator.scala` ~lines 969–974 (`validateOffsetCommit`)
- **Description**: When `generationId < 0 AND memberId == UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID AND groupInstanceId`
  is None **but the group is not Empty**, `validateOffsetCommit` returns
  `Some(Errors.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID)`.  This prevents external tools from silently clobbering
  offsets for a live consumer group.

  The existing `testCommitOffsetWithDefaultGeneration` and `testCommitOffsetsAfterGroupIsEmpty`
  only cover the case where the group does not yet exist (i.e., `getGroup` returns `None`) or is
  already `Empty`.  A regression in the condition guard (`!group.is(Empty)`) would silently allow
  the write, corrupting consumer state.

- **Proposed Test**:
  ```scala
  @Test
  def testStandaloneOffsetCommitOnNonEmptyGroupReturnsUnknownMemberId(): Unit = {
    // Bring up a Stable group with one member.
    val joinResult = dynamicJoinGroup(groupId, JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID,
      protocolType, protocols)
    val assignedMemberId = joinResult.memberId
    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    val syncResult = syncGroupLeader(groupId, joinResult.generationId, assignedMemberId,
      Map(assignedMemberId -> Array[Byte]()))
    assertEquals(Errors.NONE, syncResult.error)
    assertGroupState(groupState = Stable)

    // An admin tool tries a standalone commit (DEFAULT_GENERATION_ID = -1)
    // while the group is still active.
    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    val tp = new TopicPartition("topic", 0)
    val offset = offsetAndMetadata(100L)
    val commitResult = commitOffsets(groupId,
      OffsetCommitRequest.DEFAULT_MEMBER_ID,
      OffsetCommitRequest.DEFAULT_GENERATION_ID,
      Map(tp -> offset))

    // Must be rejected because the group is non-empty.
    assertEquals(Errors.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID, commitResult(tp))

    // Original offset must not have been changed.
    val (fetchErr, partData) = groupCoordinator.handleFetchOffsets(groupId,
      requireStable = false, Some(Seq(tp)))
    assertEquals(Errors.NONE, fetchErr)
    assertEquals(Some(OffsetFetchResponse.INVALID_OFFSET), partData.get(tp).map(_.offset))
  }
  ```
- **Coverage Delta**: Exercises `handleCommitOffsets → doCommitOffsets → validateOffsetCommit`
  branch at `!isTransactional && !group.is(Empty)` (lines 969–974), confirming the guard that
  prevents silent offset corruption.

---

### Failure Mode 4: SyncGroup storeGroup Persistence Failure Triggers Rebalance

- **Risk**: HIGH
- **Code Location**: `GroupCoordinator.scala` ~lines 582–597 (`doSyncGroup`, `storeGroup` callback)
- **Description**: When the leader sends `SyncGroup`, the coordinator calls `groupManager.storeGroup`.
  If that append fails (e.g., `MESSAGE_TOO_LARGE`, `NOT_LEADER_OR_FOLLOWER`, network error), the
  code calls `resetAndPropagateAssignmentError(group, error)` and then
  `maybePrepareRebalance(group, ...)`.  This means *all* members waiting for sync assignments
  should receive the error and be asked to rejoin.

  The existing `staticMemberRejoinWithUnknownMemberIdAndChangeOfProtocolWhileSelectProtocolUnchangedPersistenceFailure`
  tests a persistence failure during `updateStaticMemberAndRebalance` (JoinGroup path), not during
  the SyncGroup `storeGroup` call.  No test verifies the SyncGroup persistence failure branch.

- **Proposed Test**:
  ```scala
  @Test
  def testSyncGroupLeaderStoreGroupFailurePropagatesErrorAndRebalances(): Unit = {
    // Two members join and complete JoinGroup.
    val firstJoin = dynamicJoinGroup(groupId, JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID,
      protocolType, protocols)
    val leaderId = firstJoin.memberId
    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    val syncResult1 = syncGroupLeader(groupId, firstJoin.generationId, leaderId,
      Map(leaderId -> Array[Byte]()))
    assertEquals(Errors.NONE, syncResult1.error)

    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    val otherJoinFuture = sendJoinGroup(groupId,
      JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID, protocolType, protocols)
    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    val leaderRejoinFuture = sendJoinGroup(groupId, leaderId, protocolType, protocols)
    val leaderRejoin = await(leaderRejoinFuture, DefaultSessionTimeout + 100)
    val otherJoin = await(otherJoinFuture, DefaultSessionTimeout + 100)
    assertEquals(Errors.NONE, leaderRejoin.error)
    assertEquals(Errors.NONE, otherJoin.error)

    // Leader sends SyncGroup, but storeGroup fails.
    val capturedArgument: Capture[scala.collection.Map[TopicPartition, PartitionResponse] => Unit] =
      EasyMock.newCapture()
    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    EasyMock.expect(replicaManager.appendRecords(
      EasyMock.anyLong(), EasyMock.anyShort(),
      internalTopicsAllowed = EasyMock.eq(true),
      origin = EasyMock.eq(AppendOrigin.Coordinator),
      EasyMock.anyObject().asInstanceOf[Map[TopicPartition, MemoryRecords]],
      EasyMock.capture(capturedArgument),
      EasyMock.anyObject().asInstanceOf[Option[ReentrantLock]],
      EasyMock.anyObject(), EasyMock.anyObject()
    )).andAnswer(new IAnswer[Unit] {
      override def answer: Unit = capturedArgument.getValue.apply(
        Map(new TopicPartition(Topic.GROUP_METADATA_TOPIC_NAME, groupPartitionId) ->
          new PartitionResponse(Errors.MESSAGE_TOO_LARGE, 0L, RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP, 0L)))
    })
    EasyMock.replay(replicaManager)

    val generation = leaderRejoin.generationId
    val followerSyncFuture = sendSyncGroupFollower(groupId, generation, otherJoin.memberId)
    val leaderSyncResult = syncGroupLeader(groupId, generation, leaderId,
      Map(leaderId -> Array[Byte](), otherJoin.memberId -> Array[Byte]()))

    // Both should receive the persistence error, causing a new rebalance.
    assertEquals(Errors.MESSAGE_TOO_LARGE, leaderSyncResult.error)
    val followerSyncResult = await(followerSyncFuture, DefaultSessionTimeout + 100)
    assertEquals(Errors.MESSAGE_TOO_LARGE, followerSyncResult.error)

    // Group should be back to PreparingRebalance.
    assertGroupState(groupState = PreparingRebalance)
  }
  ```
- **Coverage Delta**: Exercises `doSyncGroup → storeGroup callback → error branch →
  resetAndPropagateAssignmentError → propagateAssignment → maybePrepareRebalance`
  (lines 587–591 and 1162–1186).

---

### Failure Mode 5: handleDeleteOffsets on Consumer Group in PreparingRebalance / CompletingRebalance

- **Risk**: MEDIUM
- **Code Location**: `GroupCoordinator.scala` ~lines 750–755 (`handleDeleteOffsets`)
- **Description**: The offset-deletion code path for `Stable | PreparingRebalance | CompletingRebalance`
  (when `group.isConsumerGroup`) partitions the requested topic-partitions into those the group
  is subscribed to (`GROUP_SUBSCRIBED_TO_TOPIC` error) and those it is not (eligible for
  deletion).  The existing tests only exercise this path while the group is in `Stable` state
  (`testDeleteOffsetOfStableConsumerGroup`).  A group in `PreparingRebalance` or
  `CompletingRebalance` exercises the same branch with a different `subscribedTopics` computation
  (the subscription is recomputed in `initNextGeneration`), so bugs in how transient states
  affect topic-subscription tracking could go undetected.

- **Proposed Test**:
  ```scala
  @Test
  def testDeleteOffsetOfConsumerGroupDuringRebalance(): Unit = {
    val subscription = new Subscription(List("bar").asJava)
    val protocols = List(("protocol",
      ConsumerProtocol.serializeSubscription(subscription).array()))

    val joinResult = dynamicJoinGroup(groupId, JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID,
      protocolType, protocols)
    val leaderId = joinResult.memberId
    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    syncGroupLeader(groupId, joinResult.generationId, leaderId, Map.empty)

    // Commit offsets for both a subscribed and an unsubscribed topic.
    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    val subscribedTp = new TopicPartition("bar", 0)
    val unsubscribedTp = new TopicPartition("foo", 0)
    val offset = offsetAndMetadata(10)
    val commitResult = commitOffsets(groupId, leaderId, joinResult.generationId,
      Map(subscribedTp -> offset, unsubscribedTp -> offset))
    assertEquals(Errors.NONE, commitResult(subscribedTp))
    assertEquals(Errors.NONE, commitResult(unsubscribedTp))

    // Trigger a rebalance (new member joins).
    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    sendJoinGroup(groupId, JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID, protocolType, protocols)
    assertGroupState(groupState = PreparingRebalance)

    // Attempt offset deletion while in PreparingRebalance.
    val groupTopicPartition = new TopicPartition(Topic.GROUP_METADATA_TOPIC_NAME, groupPartitionId)
    val partition: Partition = EasyMock.niceMock(classOf[Partition])
    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    EasyMock.expect(replicaManager.getMagic(EasyMock.anyObject()))
      .andStubReturn(Some(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE))
    EasyMock.expect(replicaManager.getPartition(groupTopicPartition))
      .andStubReturn(HostedPartition.Online(partition))
    EasyMock.expect(replicaManager.onlinePartition(groupTopicPartition))
      .andStubReturn(Some(partition))
    EasyMock.replay(replicaManager, partition)

    val (groupErr, topicErrors) = groupCoordinator.handleDeleteOffsets(groupId,
      Seq(subscribedTp, unsubscribedTp), RequestLocal.NoCaching)

    assertEquals(Errors.NONE, groupErr)
    // Subscribed topic should be rejected even during rebalance.
    assertEquals(Some(Errors.GROUP_SUBSCRIBED_TO_TOPIC), topicErrors.get(subscribedTp))
    // Unsubscribed topic offset should be deleted.
    assertEquals(Some(Errors.NONE), topicErrors.get(unsubscribedTp))
  }
  ```
- **Coverage Delta**: Exercises `handleDeleteOffsets → group.currentState match → PreparingRebalance`
  branch (line 750), verifying subscription-awareness during transient rebalance states.

---

### Failure Mode 6: handleDeletedPartitions — Zero Test Coverage

- **Risk**: MEDIUM
- **Code Location**: `GroupCoordinator.scala` ~lines 1064–1068 (`handleDeletedPartitions`)
- **Description**: `handleDeletedPartitions` is the broker's hook for Kafka topic partition
  deletion (`--delete-topic`).  It calls `groupManager.cleanupGroupMetadata` on *all* current
  groups to remove offsets associated with the deleted partitions.  There is no test in
  `GroupCoordinatorTest` (or in `GroupCoordinatorConcurrencyTest`) that calls this method.
  Regressions could silently leave stale offset data or crash due to a null-dereference inside
  `cleanupGroupMetadata` when invoked during an active rebalance.

- **Proposed Test**:
  ```scala
  @Test
  def testHandleDeletedPartitionsRemovesOffsetsForAllGroups(): Unit = {
    // Group 1 (groupId) — stable with committed offsets.
    val joinResult = dynamicJoinGroup(groupId, JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID,
      protocolType, protocols)
    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    syncGroupLeader(groupId, joinResult.generationId, joinResult.memberId,
      Map(joinResult.memberId -> Array[Byte]()))

    val tp1 = new TopicPartition("topic-to-delete", 0)
    val tp2 = new TopicPartition("topic-to-keep", 0)
    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    val commitResult = commitOffsets(groupId, joinResult.memberId, joinResult.generationId,
      Map(tp1 -> offsetAndMetadata(5L), tp2 -> offsetAndMetadata(10L)))
    assertEquals(Errors.NONE, commitResult(tp1))
    assertEquals(Errors.NONE, commitResult(tp2))

    // Add a second group with a standalone offset commit.
    groupCoordinator.groupManager.addPartitionOwnership(
      groupCoordinator.partitionFor(otherGroupId))
    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    commitOffsets(otherGroupId, OffsetCommitRequest.DEFAULT_MEMBER_ID,
      OffsetCommitRequest.DEFAULT_GENERATION_ID, Map(tp1 -> offsetAndMetadata(7L)))

    // Delete tp1 across all groups.
    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    groupCoordinator.handleDeletedPartitions(Seq(tp1), RequestLocal.NoCaching)

    // tp1 offsets should be gone from both groups.
    val (err1, data1) = groupCoordinator.handleFetchOffsets(groupId,
      requireStable = false, Some(Seq(tp1, tp2)))
    assertEquals(Errors.NONE, err1)
    assertEquals(Some(OffsetFetchResponse.INVALID_OFFSET), data1.get(tp1).map(_.offset))
    assertEquals(Some(10L), data1.get(tp2).map(_.offset))

    val (err2, data2) = groupCoordinator.handleFetchOffsets(otherGroupId,
      requireStable = false, Some(Seq(tp1)))
    assertEquals(Errors.NONE, err2)
    assertEquals(Some(OffsetFetchResponse.INVALID_OFFSET), data2.get(tp1).map(_.offset))
  }
  ```
- **Coverage Delta**: First test to call `handleDeletedPartitions` at all.  Exercises
  `cleanupGroupMetadata` iterating over `groupManager.currentGroups` and calling
  `_.removeOffsets(topicPartitions)` on each group, plus the offset-sensor counter increment.

---

## File References

| File | Lines |
|------|-------|
| `core/src/main/scala/kafka/coordinator/group/GroupCoordinator.scala` | 1–1,681 |
| `core/src/main/scala/kafka/coordinator/group/GroupMetadata.scala` | 1–750 |
| `core/src/test/scala/unit/kafka/coordinator/group/GroupCoordinatorTest.scala` | 1–4,150 |

### Key Source Locations by Failure Mode

| # | Method | Approx. Lines |
|---|--------|---------------|
| 1 | `onGroupUnloaded` — PreparingRebalance branch | 1,098–1,122 |
| 2 | `onGroupLoaded` — `groupIsOverCapacity` guard | 1,124–1,134 |
| 3 | `validateOffsetCommit` — non-Empty standalone guard | 969–974 |
| 4 | `doSyncGroup` — `storeGroup` error callback | 582–598 |
| 5 | `handleDeleteOffsets` — transient rebalance states | 750–755 |
| 6 | `handleDeletedPartitions` | 1,064–1,068 |

  `Some(Errors.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID)`.  This prevents external tools from silently clobbering

- **Proposed Test**:
  ```scala
  @Test
  def testStandaloneOffsetCommitOnNonEmptyGroupReturnsUnknownMemberId(): Unit = {

    val joinResult = dynamicJoinGroup(groupId, JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID,
      protocolType, protocols)

      Map(assignedMemberId -> Array[Byte]()))
    assertEquals(Errors.NONE, syncResult.error)
    assertGroupState(groupState = Stable)

    // An admin tool tries a standalone commit (DEFAULT_GENERATION_ID = -1)
    // while the group is still active.
    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    val tp = new TopicPartition("topic", 0)
    val offset = offsetAndMetadata(100L)
    val commitResult = commitOffsets(groupId,
      OffsetCommitRequest.DEFAULT_MEMBER_ID,
      OffsetCommitRequest.DEFAULT_GENERATION_ID,
      Map(tp -> offset))

    // Must be rejected because the group is non-empty.
    assertEquals(Errors.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID, commitResult(tp))

    // Original offset must not have been changed.
    val (fetchErr, partData) = groupCoordinator.handleFetchOffsets(groupId,
      requireStable = false, Some(Seq(tp)))
    assertEquals(Errors.NONE, fetchErr)
    assertEquals(Some(OffsetFetchResponse.INVALID_OFFSET), partData.get(tp).map(_.offset))
  ```

- **Coverage Delta**: Exercises `handleCommitOffsets → doCommitOffsets → validateOffsetCommit`
  branch at `!isTransactional && !group.is(Empty)` (lines 969–974), confirming the guard that
  prevents silent offset corruption.

### Failure Mode 4: SyncGroup storeGroup Persistence Failure Triggers Rebalance

- **Risk**: HIGH
- **Code Location**: `GroupCoordinator.scala` ~lines 582–597 (`doSyncGroup`, `storeGroup` callback)
- **Description**: When the leader sends `SyncGroup`, the coordinator calls `groupManager.storeGroup`.
  If that append fails (e.g., `MESSAGE_TOO_LARGE`, `NOT_LEADER_OR_FOLLOWER`, network error), the
  code calls `resetAndPropagateAssignmentError(group, error)` and then
  `maybePrepareRebalance(group, ...)`.  This means *all* members waiting for sync assignments
  should receive the error and be asked to rejoin.

  The existing `staticMemberRejoinWithUnknownMemberIdAndChangeOfProtocolWhileSelectProtocolUnchangedPersistenceFailure`
  tests a persistence failure during `updateStaticMemberAndRebalance` (JoinGroup path), not during
  the SyncGroup `storeGroup` call.  No test verifies the SyncGroup persistence failure branch.

- **Proposed Test**:
  ```scala
  @Test
  def testSyncGroupLeaderStoreGroupFailurePropagatesErrorAndRebalances(): Unit = {
    // Two members join and complete JoinGroup.
    val firstJoin = dynamicJoinGroup(groupId, JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID,
      protocolType, protocols)
    val leaderId = firstJoin.memberId
    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    val syncResult1 = syncGroupLeader(groupId, firstJoin.generationId, leaderId,
      Map(leaderId -> Array[Byte]()))
    assertEquals(Errors.NONE, syncResult1.error)

    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    val otherJoinFuture = sendJoinGroup(groupId,
      JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID, protocolType, protocols)
    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    val leaderRejoinFuture = sendJoinGroup(groupId, leaderId, protocolType, protocols)
    val leaderRejoin = await(leaderRejoinFuture, DefaultSessionTimeout + 100)
    val otherJoin = await(otherJoinFuture, DefaultSessionTimeout + 100)
    assertEquals(Errors.NONE, leaderRejoin.error)
    assertEquals(Errors.NONE, otherJoin.error)

    // Leader sends SyncGroup, but storeGroup fails.
    val capturedArgument: Capture[scala.collection.Map[TopicPartition, PartitionResponse] => Unit] =
      EasyMock.newCapture()
    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    EasyMock.expect(replicaManager.appendRecords(
      EasyMock.anyLong(), EasyMock.anyShort(),
      internalTopicsAllowed = EasyMock.eq(true),
      origin = EasyMock.eq(AppendOrigin.Coordinator),
      EasyMock.anyObject().asInstanceOf[Map[TopicPartition, MemoryRecords]],
      EasyMock.capture(capturedArgument),
      EasyMock.anyObject().asInstanceOf[Option[ReentrantLock]],
      EasyMock.anyObject(), EasyMock.anyObject()
    )).andAnswer(new IAnswer[Unit] {
      override def answer: Unit = capturedArgument.getValue.apply(
        Map(new TopicPartition(Topic.GROUP_METADATA_TOPIC_NAME, groupPartitionId) ->
          new PartitionResponse(Errors.MESSAGE_TOO_LARGE, 0L, RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP, 0L)))
    })
    EasyMock.replay(replicaManager)

    val generation = leaderRejoin.generationId
    val followerSyncFuture = sendSyncGroupFollower(groupId, generation, otherJoin.memberId)
    val leaderSyncResult = syncGroupLeader(groupId, generation, leaderId,
      Map(leaderId -> Array[Byte](), otherJoin.memberId -> Array[Byte]()))

    // Both should receive the persistence error, causing a new rebalance.
    assertEquals(Errors.MESSAGE_TOO_LARGE, leaderSyncResult.error)
    val followerSyncResult = await(followerSyncFuture, DefaultSessionTimeout + 100)
    assertEquals(Errors.MESSAGE_TOO_LARGE, followerSyncResult.error)

    // Group should be back to PreparingRebalance.
    assertGroupState(groupState = PreparingRebalance)
  }
  ```
- **Coverage Delta**: Exercises `doSyncGroup → storeGroup callback → error branch →
  resetAndPropagateAssignmentError → propagateAssignment → maybePrepareRebalance`
  (lines 587–591 and 1162–1186).

---

### Failure Mode 5: handleDeleteOffsets on Consumer Group in PreparingRebalance / CompletingRebalance

- **Risk**: MEDIUM
- **Code Location**: `GroupCoordinator.scala` ~lines 750–755 (`handleDeleteOffsets`)
- **Description**: The offset-deletion code path for `Stable | PreparingRebalance | CompletingRebalance`
  (when `group.isConsumerGroup`) partitions the requested topic-partitions into those the group
  is subscribed to (`GROUP_SUBSCRIBED_TO_TOPIC` error) and those it is not (eligible for
  deletion).  The existing tests only exercise this path while the group is in `Stable` state
  (`testDeleteOffsetOfStableConsumerGroup`).  A group in `PreparingRebalance` or
  `CompletingRebalance` exercises the same branch with a different `subscribedTopics` computation
  (the subscription is recomputed in `initNextGeneration`), so bugs in how transient states
  affect topic-subscription tracking could go undetected.

- **Proposed Test**:
  ```scala
  @Test
  def testDeleteOffsetOfConsumerGroupDuringRebalance(): Unit = {
    val subscription = new Subscription(List("bar").asJava)
    val protocols = List(("protocol",
      ConsumerProtocol.serializeSubscription(subscription).array()))

    val joinResult = dynamicJoinGroup(groupId, JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID,
      protocolType, protocols)
    val leaderId = joinResult.memberId
    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    syncGroupLeader(groupId, joinResult.generationId, leaderId, Map.empty)

    // Commit offsets for both a subscribed and an unsubscribed topic.
    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    val subscribedTp = new TopicPartition("bar", 0)
    val unsubscribedTp = new TopicPartition("foo", 0)
    val offset = offsetAndMetadata(10)
    val commitResult = commitOffsets(groupId, leaderId, joinResult.generationId,
      Map(subscribedTp -> offset, unsubscribedTp -> offset))
    assertEquals(Errors.NONE, commitResult(subscribedTp))
    assertEquals(Errors.NONE, commitResult(unsubscribedTp))

    // Trigger a rebalance (new member joins).
    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    sendJoinGroup(groupId, JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID, protocolType, protocols)
    assertGroupState(groupState = PreparingRebalance)

    // Attempt offset deletion while in PreparingRebalance.
    val groupTopicPartition = new TopicPartition(Topic.GROUP_METADATA_TOPIC_NAME, groupPartitionId)
    val partition: Partition = EasyMock.niceMock(classOf[Partition])
    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    EasyMock.expect(replicaManager.getMagic(EasyMock.anyObject()))
      .andStubReturn(Some(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE))
    EasyMock.expect(replicaManager.getPartition(groupTopicPartition))
      .andStubReturn(HostedPartition.Online(partition))
    EasyMock.expect(replicaManager.onlinePartition(groupTopicPartition))
      .andStubReturn(Some(partition))
    EasyMock.replay(replicaManager, partition)

    val (groupErr, topicErrors) = groupCoordinator.handleDeleteOffsets(groupId,
      Seq(subscribedTp, unsubscribedTp), RequestLocal.NoCaching)

    assertEquals(Errors.NONE, groupErr)
    // Subscribed topic should be rejected even during rebalance.
    assertEquals(Some(Errors.GROUP_SUBSCRIBED_TO_TOPIC), topicErrors.get(subscribedTp))
    // Unsubscribed topic offset should be deleted.
    assertEquals(Some(Errors.NONE), topicErrors.get(unsubscribedTp))
  }
  ```
- **Coverage Delta**: Exercises `handleDeleteOffsets → group.currentState match → PreparingRebalance`
  branch (line 750), verifying subscription-awareness during transient rebalance states.

---

### Failure Mode 6: handleDeletedPartitions — Zero Test Coverage

- **Risk**: MEDIUM
- **Code Location**: `GroupCoordinator.scala` ~lines 1064–1068 (`handleDeletedPartitions`)
- **Description**: `handleDeletedPartitions` is the broker's hook for Kafka topic partition
  deletion (`--delete-topic`).  It calls `groupManager.cleanupGroupMetadata` on *all* current
  groups to remove offsets associated with the deleted partitions.  There is no test in
  `GroupCoordinatorTest` (or in `GroupCoordinatorConcurrencyTest`) that calls this method.
  Regressions could silently leave stale offset data or crash due to a null-dereference inside
  `cleanupGroupMetadata` when invoked during an active rebalance.

- **Proposed Test**:
  ```scala
  @Test
  def testHandleDeletedPartitionsRemovesOffsetsForAllGroups(): Unit = {
    // Group 1 (groupId) — stable with committed offsets.
    val joinResult = dynamicJoinGroup(groupId, JoinGroupRequest.UNKNOWN_MEMBER_ID,
      protocolType, protocols)
    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    syncGroupLeader(groupId, joinResult.generationId, joinResult.memberId,
      Map(joinResult.memberId -> Array[Byte]()))

    val tp1 = new TopicPartition("topic-to-delete", 0)
    val tp2 = new TopicPartition("topic-to-keep", 0)
    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    val commitResult = commitOffsets(groupId, joinResult.memberId, joinResult.generationId,
      Map(tp1 -> offsetAndMetadata(5L), tp2 -> offsetAndMetadata(10L)))
    assertEquals(Errors.NONE, commitResult(tp1))
    assertEquals(Errors.NONE, commitResult(tp2))

    // Add a second group with a standalone offset commit.
    groupCoordinator.groupManager.addPartitionOwnership(
      groupCoordinator.partitionFor(otherGroupId))
    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    commitOffsets(otherGroupId, OffsetCommitRequest.DEFAULT_MEMBER_ID,
      OffsetCommitRequest.DEFAULT_GENERATION_ID, Map(tp1 -> offsetAndMetadata(7L)))

    // Delete tp1 across all groups.
    EasyMock.reset(replicaManager)
    groupCoordinator.handleDeletedPartitions(Seq(tp1), RequestLocal.NoCaching)

    // tp1 offsets should be gone from both groups.
    val (err1, data1) = groupCoordinator.handleFetchOffsets(groupId,
      requireStable = false, Some(Seq(tp1, tp2)))
    assertEquals(Errors.NONE, err1)
    assertEquals(Some(OffsetFetchResponse.INVALID_OFFSET), data1.get(tp1).map(_.offset))
    assertEquals(Some(10L), data1.get(tp2).map(_.offset))

    val (err2, data2) = groupCoordinator.handleFetchOffsets(otherGroupId,
      requireStable = false, Some(Seq(tp1)))
    assertEquals(Errors.NONE, err2)
    assertEquals(Some(OffsetFetchResponse.INVALID_OFFSET), data2.get(tp1).map(_.offset))
  }
  ```
- **Coverage Delta**: First test to call `handleDeletedPartitions` at all.  Exercises
  `cleanupGroupMetadata` iterating over `groupManager.currentGroups` and calling
  `_.removeOffsets(topicPartitions)` on each group, plus the offset-sensor counter increment.
